audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Herod persecution, James killed, Peter imprisoned and angel-released, prayer house of Mary, Rhoda, Herod's death, word growth.

verses.restored: At that time Herod the king laid hands on some from the assembly to do harm.
He killed James the brother of John with a sword.
Seeing that it was pleasing to the Judeans, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. These were the days of Unleavened Bread.
Seizing him, he put him in prison, handing him over to four squads of four soldiers to guard him, intending after the Passover to bring him up to the people.
So Peter was being kept in the prison, but prayer was being made earnestly by the assembly to the Divine concerning him.
When Herod was about to bring him forward, that night Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and guards before the door were keeping the prison.
Look, a messenger of the Lord stood by, and light shone in the cell. Striking Peter's side, he awakened him, saying, Rise quickly. And the chains fell from his hands.
The messenger said to him, Belt yourself and tie on your sandals. He did so. And he says to him, Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.
Going out, he was following, and he did not know that what was happening through the messenger was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.

Passing the first guard and the second, they came to the iron gate leading into the city, which opened to them by itself. Going out, they went forward one street, and immediately the messenger departed from him.
Peter, coming to himself, said, Now I know truly that the Lord sent his messenger and rescued me from Herod's hand and all the expectation of the Judean people.
Knowing this, he came to the house of Mary, the mother of John called Mark, where many were gathered together and praying.
When he knocked at the door of the gate, a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer.
Recognizing Peter's voice, because of joy she did not open the gate, but running in, reported that Peter stood before the gate.
They said to her, You are mad. But she kept insisting it was so. They were saying, It is his messenger.
But Peter continued knocking. Opening, they saw him and were amazed.

Motioning to them with his hand to be silent, he described to them how the Lord brought him out of the prison. He said, Report these things to James and to the siblings. And going out, he went to another place.
When day came, there was no small disturbance among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
Herod, searching for him and not finding him, examined the guards and commanded them to be led away. Going down from Judea to Caesarea, he stayed there.

He was angrily disposed toward the Tyrians and Sidonians. They came to him with one accord, and persuading Blastus, the one over the king's bedchamber, they were asking for peace, because their country was fed from the king's country.
On an appointed day Herod, putting on royal clothing and sitting on the judgment seat, was delivering a public address to them.
The people were crying out, A god's voice and not a human's.
Immediately a messenger of the Lord struck him because he did not give the glory to the Divine, and being eaten by worms, he breathed out.
But the word of the Divine grew and multiplied.
Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem, having fulfilled the service, taking along John who was called Mark.
